title: Cancrinia pjataevae

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cancrinia pjataevae include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Cancrinia pjataevae is used in herbal medicine, often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, to take advantage of its health benefits and to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
